name: meiosis

phase_0.boundary_condition: The plasma membrane and extracellular matrix constraints that contain the four final haploid products.

VINIT.chosen_element: Primary diploid germ cell entering meiotic prophase
VINIT.justification: Represents the uninitialized diploid state before meiotic commitment and DNA replication.

TANCH.chosen_element: Four haploid gametes enclosed by plasma membranes
TANCH.justification: Defines the closed boundary containing the final haploid products ready for fertilization.

AFWD.chosen_element: Cyclin-dependent kinase cascade driving meiotic progression
AFWD.justification: Propels the cell forward through the sequential phases of meiosis I and II.

AREV.chosen_element: Reduction of ploidy and DNA content
AREV.justification: Reverses the diploid state to haploidy by separating homologous chromosomes and sister chromatids.

CLINK.chosen_element: Sequential execution of meiotic phases and cytokinesis
CLINK.justification: Chains the distinct meiotic divisions and cytoplasmic separations into a continuous lineage.

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: Homologous chromosome recognition via sequence homology
IMSCRIB.justification: Allows chromosomes to identify and align with their exact homologous partners.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Spo11-induced double-strand breaks branching into recombination intermediates
FSPLIT.justification: Splits the intact DNA duplex into distinct crossover and non-crossover repair paths.

FFUSE.chosen_element: Holliday junction resolvase and DNA ligase reconstituting intact chromosomes
FFUSE.justification: Fuses the recombining DNA strands back into continuous chromosome structures.

EVALT.chosen_element: Successful crossover designation and chiasma formation
EVALT.justification: Affirms the successful formation of physical connections between homologous chromosomes.

EVALF.justification: Negates the crossover path by resolving intermediates without exchanging flanking markers.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: Bivalent tetrad held in metaphase I tension
ENGAGR.justification: Holds the homologous chromosomes in a paradice of physical connection and genetic distinctness.

IFIX.chosen_element: Epigenetic reprogramming and permanent genetic recombination record
IFIX.justification: Fixes the haploid genome state and crossover locations as an append-only record.

phase_2.fuse_element: Holliday junction resolvase and DNA ligase

phase_3.void_description: Uninitialized diploid state before meiotic commitment
phase_3.true_description: Haploid gamete with successful recombination and intact membranes
phase_3.false_description: Aneuploid gamete or apoptotic cell due to unrepaired double-strand breaks
phase_3.both_description: Bivalent tetrad held in metaphase I tension, physically connected yet genetically distinct

steps.domain_action: Initialize the primary diploid germ cell entering meiotic prophase.
Homologous chromosomes recognize and align via sequence homology.
Cyclin-dependent kinase cascade drives progression into leptotene and zygotene.
Spo11 induces double-strand breaks, splitting the DNA duplex into recombining strands.
Anchor the T-arm with successful crossover designation.
Chain the strand invasion and Holliday junction formation.
Anchor the F-arm with non-crossover synthesis.
Reverse the DNA ends via non-crossover ligation and repair.
Resolvase and DNA ligase fuse the strands, reconstituting the intact homologous chromosome pair.
The synaptonemal complex disassembles, leaving the bivalent tetrad held in metaphase I tension.
Anaphase I forward morphism pulls homologous chromosomes to opposite poles.
Cytokinesis I completes the first division, chaining into meiosis II.
Anaphase II separates sister chromatids, completing the reductional descent to haploidy.
Epigenetic imprinting fixes the permanent genetic recombination record in the haploid genome.
Telophase II and cytokinesis close the boundary, yielding four distinct haploid gametes.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Cyclin-dependent kinase complexes and meiotic checkpoint kinases
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Synaptonemal complex lateral elements and transverse filaments
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Chromatin epigenetic marks and cohesin ring complexes
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Spindle assembly checkpoint and anaphase-promoting complex
phase_5.alfs_store: The reference diploid genome and meiotic recombination hotspot map

phase_6.cycle_cost: ATP hydrolysis for DNA double-strand break repair and chromatin remodeling
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Diploid germ cell with intact homologous chromosomes
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Four haploid gametes with recombined genomes
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The genetic information is conserved and restructured without loss, as the topological recombination cycle is a Frobenius-verified identity operation on the genome.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → IMSCRIB → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → CLINK → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → ENGAGR → AFWD → CLINK → AREV → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: meiosis
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=15
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(3, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑠  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑼 → 𐑾  | identity — self-imscription
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑠 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [4] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [5] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [6] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [7] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑾  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [10] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑳 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [11] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑗  | composition — regime coherence
--   [12] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑱 → 𐑭  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [13] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑗 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [14] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def meiosis_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the primary diploid germ cell entering meiotic prophase.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Homologous chromosomes recognize and align via sequence homology.)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑠 𐑚)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Cyclin-dependent kinase cascade drives progression into leptotene and zygotene.)
  -- FSPLIT [3] (gran := 𐑚) (Spo11 induces double-strand breaks, splitting the DNA duplex into recombining strands.) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Anchor the T-arm with successful crossover designation.)
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain the strand invasion and Holliday junction formation.)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Anchor the F-arm with non-crossover synthesis.)
        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Reverse the DNA ends via non-crossover ligation and repair.))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Resolvase and DNA ligase fuse the strands, reconstituting the intact homologous chromosome pair.)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑾)  -- [9] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (The synaptonemal complex disassembles, leaving the bivalent tetrad held in me...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑳 𐑱)  -- [10] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Anaphase I forward morphism pulls homologous chromosomes to opposite poles.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑗)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Cytokinesis I completes the first division, chaining into meiosis II.)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [12] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Anaphase II separates sister chromatids, completing the reductional descent t...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑗 𐑡)  -- [13] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Epigenetic imprinting fixes the permanent genetic recombination record in the...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [14] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Telophase II and cytokinesis close the boundary, yielding four distinct haplo...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def meiosis_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (meiosis_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def meiosis_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (meiosis_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem meiosis_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
